Aider-AI/aider#426.body:
When playing with `aider` from command line ( https://aider.chat/docs/faq.html#can-i-script-aider ):

```
aider --message-file PROMPT.FILE  #AKA aider -f PROMPT.FILE
```

I've encountered situation that it asked me if to "add files FOO BAR ?" 

and this stopped my automation obviously ;).

Any ideas how we could tackle this?

// Btw. here is small template for those that want to experiment with trees of generations: https://github.com/CLIAI/hello_world_three_of_generations_aider_CLI    ; when I was playing with it I encountered at some point above situation in one of generations.
Aider-AI/aider#426.title: [scripting][`--message-file`] Aider asks in interactive way. Feature request:  `--non-interactive` or `AIDER_NON_INTERACTIVE=true` support for scripting AIDER from Command Line

Aider-AI/aider#3785.body:
### Issue

Today I find that gemini is not writing changes to .sh files. It suggests edits, and says it has made them; I can see the diff output, but nothing happens. 
If I want it to create a new file, therefore doing /add new_file.sh, it remains empty. 

Example:

```
Readonly: aider_prompt.md                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
architect> /add test_hello.sh                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
 
No files matched 'test_hello.sh'. Do you want to create /home/tim/django_api_sync/test_hello.sh? (Y)es/(N)o [Yes]: y                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
Added test_hello.sh to the chat

Readonly: aider_prompt.md
Editable: test_hello.sh                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
architect> make test_hello.sh accept one command line argument, and echo it back                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
 
Okay, Editor Engineer, please apply the following changes to test_hello.sh.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       

Change Request:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   

Modify the test_hello.sh script to:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               

 1 Expect exactly one command-line argument.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
 2 Print the provided argument to standard output.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
 3 If the number of arguments is incorrect, print a usage message to standard error and exit with a non-zero status code.                                                                                                                                                                                                                         

Instructions:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     

Add the following content to the test_hello.sh file.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              

Code Changes:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
#!/bin/bash                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
# Check if exactly one argument is provided                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       
if [ "$#" -ne 1 ]; then

fi                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
# Echo the first argument                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
echo "$1"                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
exit 0
```

Aider-AI/aider#4109.body:
### Issue

Using the Vertex AI provider/platform/API, we've been getting this series of errors forever, and I think it's causing fallback to the "big expensive" model for every operation, which is kinda burning through our free credits really fast (and it's slower, etc).

Usage:

```bash
VERTEXAI_PROJECT=XXX VERTEXAI_LOCATION=us-central1 aider --no-gitignore --no-attribute-author --no-attribute-committer --model vertex_ai/gemini-2.5-pro-preview-05-06
```

The `litellm.BadRequestError` shows up on every operation:

```
Applied edit to TODO.md
litellm.BadRequestError: LLM Provider NOT provided. Pass in the LLM provider you are trying to call. You passed model=vertex_ai-language-models/gemini-2.5-flash-preview-04-17
 Pass model as E.g. For 'Huggingface' inference endpoints pass in `completion(model='huggingface/starcoder',..)` Learn more: https://docs.litellm.ai/docs/providers
```

Aider suggests this fix to aider and it appears to work for me. Feel free to add it back (I don't know if this is actually the best/correct place for this fix or if it should be fixed at the provider/model name creation elsewhere):


## Session

Okay, I will modify aider/models.py to normalize the Vertex AI model names. The change involves checking if the model name contains "-language-models/" and starts with   
vertex_ai, and if so, replacing "-language-models/" with / to ensure LiteLLM can correctly parse the provider. This normalization will happen in the Model class          
constructor.                                                                                                                                                              

Here is the SEARCH/REPLACE block:                                                                                                                                         

```haskell
aider/models.py                                                                                                                                                           
<<<<<<< SEARCH                                                                                                                                                            
        # Map any alias to its canonical name                                                                                                                             
        model = MODEL_ALIASES.get(model, model)                                                                                                                           
                                                                                                                                                                          
        self.name = model                                                                                                                                                 
=======                                                                                                                                                                   
        # Map any alias to its canonical name                                                                                                                             
        model = MODEL_ALIASES.get(model, model)                                                                                                                           
                                                                                                                                                                          
        # Normalize Vertex AI model names that include "-language-models/"                                                                                                
        if model.startswith("vertex_ai") and "-language-models/" in model:                                                                                                
            model = model.replace("-language-models/", "/", 1)                                                                                                            
                                                                                                                                                                          
        self.name = model                                                                                                                                                 
>>>>>>> REPLACE
```



### Version and model info (with the above fix)

```haskell
Aider v0.83.3.dev26+g07c5e3d2
Main model: vertex_ai/gemini-2.5-pro-preview-05-06 with diff-fenced edit format
Weak model: vertex_ai/gemini-2.5-flash-preview-04-17
Repo-map: using 4096 tokens, auto refresh
```

### Version and model info (without the above fix)

```haskell
Main model: vertex_ai/gemini-2.5-pro-preview-05-06 with diff-fenced edit format
Weak model: vertex_ai-language-models/gemini-2.5-flash-preview-04-17
Repo-map: using 4096 tokens, auto refresh
```
Aider-AI/aider#4109.title: Vertex AI models can't use the weak or editor models?

Aider-AI/aider#4298.body:
### Issue

When models with a duplicate names exist in aider's model-settings.yml, custom model settings defined in `~/.aider.model.settings.yml` fail to override the duplicated models configuration.

Duplicate model names found:
* `azure/o4-mini`
* `openai/o4-mini`
* `openrouter/openai/o4-mini`
* `vertex_ai/claude-3-7-sonnet@20250219`
* `vertex_ai/claude-opus-4@20250514`
* `vertex_ai/claude-sonnet-4@20250514`

### Reproduction
1. Choose a model with duplicate entries in `model-settings.yml`.
2. Add custom settings for that model in `~/.aider.model.settings.yml`
3. Attempt to use the model with custom settings
4. Observe that default settings are used instead of custom overrides

**Example:**
Consider trying to override settings for `openai/o4-mini` in `~/.aider.model.settings.yml`:
                                                                                           
```yaml
- name: openai/o4-mini
  edit_format: whole  # changed
  weak_model_name: openai/gpt-4.1-mini
  use_repo_map: false  # changed
  examples_as_sys_msg: true
  use_temperature: false
  editor_model_name: openai/gpt-4.1
  editor_edit_format: editor-diff
  system_prompt_prefix: 'Formatting re-enabled. '
  accepts_settings:
  - reasoning_effort
```

**Expected behavior:** The custom settings should be applied to `openai/o4-mini` when selected. Aider should use the "whole" edit format and shouldn't use repo map.

**Actual behavior:** The custom settings are ignored, and the model uses default settings instead. Edit format is still `diff`, and the repo map is used.
                                                              
### Root cause
If model with the same name appears multiple times in the model-settings.yml, the override mechanism removes the first model instance, adds a new instance to the end of model registry, but the actual one which is used would the second one from `model-settings.yml`.

### Impact
- Users cannot customize model behavior through configuration files
- Inconsistent behavior between expected and actual model settings

### Version and model info

Aider v0.85.1
Main model: openai/o4-mini with diff edit format
Weak model: openai/gpt-4.1-mini
Git repo: .git with 54 files
Repo-map: using 4096 tokens, auto refresh
Aider-AI/aider#4298.title: Duplicate Models Prevent Model Settings Overrides from Working
